summary: Task 17 adds 7 ChatRepositoryTest cases and 8 ChatViewModelTest cases covering the Draft staging path, the Persistent savePersistentAttachment path, the send-gate on in-flight staging, and the hard-gate on persistent save failure. Most new tests pin the right invariants (the send-gate test in particular builds a delegating ChatRepository with a CompletableDeferred to model the race cleanly, and the commit test asserts the final Room imagePath + final on-disk file). Two meaningful gaps remain: (1) `draftMode_removeAttachment_deletesStagedFile` is structurally under-asserted â€” it only checks the list is empty and has no way to observe the staged-file delete because FakeChatRepository exposes no delete hook and the fake never wrote a real file to disk; (2) the TDD anchor `writeAttachmentStaging_failedWrite_rollsBackPartialFile` is absent even though the production `writeAttachmentPayload` implements the rollback (target.delete() in catch) â€” the behaviour is untested. Secondary gaps: no containment-check test for `savePersistentAttachment` (production only checks containment for `writeAttachmentStaging`), no coverage of persistent-mode audio attach, and the in-flight-send test carries ~40 lines of abandoned-approach commentary that should be trimmed. No existing Phase-2 test was invalidated by the `Attachment.Audio.equals/hashCode` change (all Quick-mode sites leave `stagedFilename = null` on both sides of any equality check).

findings.issue: Test name promises it verifies the staged file is deleted on removeAttachment, but the only assertion is `vm.attachments.value.isEmpty()`. Litmus test fails: if `deleteStagedFileIfAny` is stripped from production (the `viewModelScope.launch(Dispatchers.IO) { target.delete() }` block), this test still passes â€” the list is already cleared by `_attachments.update` before the delete coroutine runs. The TDD anchor in 17.md explicitly asked for an assertion against `FakeChatRepository.deletedStagingFiles`, but the fake does not expose a delete hook, and the real `File.delete()` fires on a file the fake never wrote (fake `writeAttachmentStaging` returns a filename without performing I/O).
findings.recommendation: Either (a) extend FakeChatRepository to record delete calls by adding a `deletedStagingFiles: MutableList<String>` and exposing a `deleteStagedFile(stagingDir, filename)` method that ChatViewModel calls through the repository instead of directly invoking `java.io.File.delete()`; or (b) keep the direct File.delete() in production and make the test actually write a file first, then assert it is gone. Concrete option (a): refactor ChatViewModel.deleteStagedFileIfAny to call `chatRepository.deleteStagedFile(dir, filename)`; the fake records into `deletedStagingFiles`; the test asserts `assertEquals(listOf("img_0.png"), fakeChatRepository.deletedStagingFiles)`. Concrete option (b): in the test, after `addImageBitmap` + `advanceUntilIdle`, manually `File(ensureDraftStagingDirReflected, "img_0.png").writeBytes(...)` to seed the file, then `removeAttachment(0)`, then `assertFalse(File(...).exists())`. Option (a) is cleaner and symmetric with how every other staging operation is tested.

findings.issue: TDD anchor `writeAttachmentStaging_failedWrite_rollsBackPartialFile` from 17.md line 95 is missing. Production `DefaultChatRepository.writeAttachmentPayload` wraps the write in try/catch and calls `target.delete()` on failure so the next `nextAttachmentFilename` does not pick up a half-written stub as N=0 (explicit comment on line 188 of the diff). This rollback is load-bearing: without it a failed write would make sequential indices non-monotonic. Not tested.
findings.recommendation: Add a Robolectric test that simulates a failed Bitmap.compress. Options: (1) construct an `Attachment.Image` with a recycled Bitmap (`bmp.recycle()` before passing) â€” `Bitmap.compress` returns false, production throws and must delete the partial file. Assert `dir.listFiles()` is empty after the throw. (2) Alternatively, wrap the write dispatch behind an injectable payload writer and swap in a throwing one. Suggested test body: `val bmp = Bitmap.createBitmap(1,1,Bitmap.Config.ARGB_8888).also { it.recycle() }; val thrown = runCatching { repo.writeAttachmentStaging(staging, Attachment.Image(bmp)) }.exceptionOrNull(); assertTrue(thrown is IOException); assertTrue("partial file must be deleted", staging.listFiles().isNullOrEmpty()); val next = repo.writeAttachmentStaging(staging, Attachment.Image(goodBmp)); assertEquals("img_0.png", next) // counter not bumped by the failure`.

findings.issue: writeAttachmentStaging has a containment-check test (`writeAttachmentStaging_stagingDirOutsideRoot_throws`) mirroring the security guarantee. `savePersistentAttachment` has no symmetric test. Looking at production DefaultChatRepository.savePersistentAttachment: it constructs `File(filesDir, "attachments/$chatId")` but does NOT call `requireInsideAttachmentsRoot` on the result. A caller passing a malicious chatId like `../..` would escape the attachments root. This is both a missing test AND an actual security hole the test would have caught.
findings.recommendation: Add `savePersistentAttachment_chatIdTraversal_throws`: invoke repo.savePersistentAttachment(chatId = -1 or with a path-traversal encoded chatId once chatId becomes String, filesDir, Attachment.Image(...)) â€” should throw IOException. Since chatId is Long the Long itself can't traverse, but the guarantee should still be explicit: after the `mkdirs`, call `requireInsideAttachmentsRoot(chatDir, filesDir)` in production and add a test that confirms a synthetic filesDir+chatId that resolves outside attachments (symlink attack â€” chatDir being a symlink to /tmp) throws. Simpler test shape: pass a filesDir whose `attachments/N` resolves outside â€” assert IOException. This closes the security-audit parity gap between the two write paths.

findings.issue: `persistentMode_sendWithImage_callsSavePersistentAttachment` covers the image branch only. Production `runInferencePersistent` has a parallel `persistedAudio?.let { chatRepository.savePersistentAttachment(...).audioPath }` branch â€” untested. Also untested: image + audio together in the same send (MVP says one of each per row; production takes `firstOrNull { it is Attachment.Image }` and `firstOrNull { it is Attachment.Audio }`).
findings.recommendation: Add `persistentMode_sendWithAudio_callsSavePersistentAttachment`: mirror the image test but with `vm.addAudio(pcm, 1000L)`; assert `fakeChatRepository.persistentAttachmentCalls.single().attachment is Attachment.Audio`; assert USER row's `audioPath == "attachments/7/audio_0.wav"` and `imagePath == null`. Optionally add `persistentMode_sendWithImageAndAudio_writesBoth` to pin the two-call contract (image first, then audio, both written to disk, USER row carries both paths).

findings.issue: No test pins two Task-17 ChatViewModel invariants called out in the task comments: (a) `draftStagingDir` is reset to null after a successful commit (line 706 of ChatViewModel: `draftStagingDir = null` inside commitDraft onSuccess) â€” a later `removeAttachment` must therefore be a no-op that does not target the final chat dir; (b) `draftStagingDir` stays null when the user never attaches anything (lazy create via `ensureDraftStagingDir`) â€” important because the startup orphan-sweep (Task 6 AC-A3) relies on .staging-* dirs only being created when needed.
findings.recommendation: Add `draftMode_commitThenRemove_doesNotCrash`: add image, send (draft commits, staging dir renamed), then directly call `vm.removeAttachment(0)` with an invalid index â€” assert no throw, no file side-effect on the now-renamed `attachments/{chatId}/` dir. Add `draftMode_noAttachment_doesNotCreateStagingDir`: build a Draft VM, advance, send text only â€” assert `context.filesDir/attachments/` contains no `.staging-*` entries (since fake doesn't create it; for true coverage, call the real DefaultChatRepository through a thin wrapper or reflect `draftStagingDir` via reflection, though reflection is brittle â€” prefer verifying the contract through the public API: after text-only send, no stagingDir was passed to commitDraftChat, which is already checked in `commitDraftChat_nullStagingDir_skipsRenameAndSucceeds` at the repo layer).

findings.issue: The test body carries ~40 lines of stale commentary describing three abandoned approaches (writeStagingError+sentinel, reflection-based injection, direct addImage with stagedFilename=null) before landing on the CompletableDeferred-delegation approach it actually uses. The final approach is sound (delegating ChatRepository + CompletableDeferred cleanly suspends the staging write and exercises the real send-gate), but the noise makes reviewers re-derive the reasoning every time they touch this file. Minor test smell, not a correctness issue.
findings.recommendation: Replace lines 527-567 with a single 3-line comment: `// Suspend the staging write with a CompletableDeferred â€” the attachment stays in _attachments with stagedFilename=null, which is exactly the in-flight state the send-gate guards against.` Keep the block.await() / block.complete() structure as-is. The test logic itself is correct and the delegating-ChatRepository pattern is the cleanest expression of this race â€” once the commentary is trimmed the test reads as intended.

findings.issue: FakeChatRepository.savePersistentAttachment returns a hard-coded `attachments/$chatId/img_0.png` / `audio_0.wav` regardless of how many times it is called for the same chatId. Real DefaultChatRepository uses `dir.listFiles().size` â†’ sequential indices (img_0, img_1, ...). No test currently calls savePersistentAttachment twice for the same chatId, but if one is added, the fake will drift from reality and silently report img_0 for the second call â€” hiding a potential filename-collision bug.
findings.recommendation: Symmetrise the fake with the `stagedImageIndex`/`stagedAudioIndex` counters already used by writeAttachmentStaging: use a `Map<Long, IndexPair>` keyed by chatId. Replace the `when` body with `val idx = persistentIndex.getOrPut(chatId) { IndexPair(0, 0) }; return when (attachment) { is Image -> { val i = idx.image++; PersistedAttachment(imagePath = "attachments/$chatId/img_$i.png") } ... }`. Then add the image+audio sequential test from the previous finding â€” it becomes a pin against that drift.

findings.issue: `Attachment.Audio.equals/hashCode` was extended with `stagedFilename`. The user flagged `addAudio_alreadyHasAudio_isNoOp` and `send_transfersAudioAttachmentAndClears` as at risk. Reviewed: both run in Quick mode where `stagedFilename` is null on both sides, and both rely on `assertSame` (reference identity) rather than `assertEquals` (value equality) â€” they are unaffected. However, if a future test constructs two `Attachment.Audio` with identical pcm/durationMs but different stagedFilenames (one staged, one not), equals correctly returns false â€” this is a new behaviour with no direct test.
findings.recommendation: Add a tiny unit test in AttachmentTest (create the file if missing): `fun audio_equals_differsByStagedFilename()`: two Audios with same pcm+durationMs, different stagedFilenames â†’ `assertNotEquals`, `assertNotEquals(hash, hash)`. Pins the equals/hashCode contract so a future refactor that reverts the stagedFilename inclusion is caught. If an AttachmentTest file does not exist, this is also fine as a two-assertion check inside ChatViewModelTest.
